title: Emerging AI Architectures — Mamba, Diffusion & Test-Time Compute
description: The AI field is transitioning beyond pure dense Transformers. Staff Engineers must evaluate emerging paradigms including State Space Models (Mamba), Diffusion-based models, and Test-Time Compute scaling architectures (search, verifiers, reasoning models).

sections.title: State Space Models: Mamba vs. Transformers
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Transformers suffer from quadratic computational complexity $O(N^2)$ due to attention computations. **State Space Models (SSMs)** like **Mamba** achieve linear complexity $O(N)$ with sequence length by reformulating sequence processing as a continuous system discretization. They maintain a constant-size hidden state (no growing KV cache memory bottlenecks) while preserving selective scanning to focus on relevant information.

bar_chart Scale Comparison: At a sequence length of 128K, Transformers require massive KV cache VRAM; Mamba models process the context with a flat memory footprint, speeding up generation speeds.
sections.codeBlock:
# Selective Scan (Mamba) conceptual calculation
def selective_scan(x, A, B, C, Delta):
    # Discretize continuous state matrices (Euler/Bilinear approximation)
    dA = np.exp(Delta * A)
    dB = Delta * B
    
    # Update hidden state: h_t = dA * h_{t-1} + dB * x_t
    # Output: y_t = C * h_t
    return dA, dB

sections.title: Diffusion Models: DDPM, Flow Matching & DiT
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: **Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPM)** generate images/audio by iteratively removing noise. Modern implementations use **Flow Matching** to trace straight paths between noise and target states, accelerating convergence. **Diffusion Transformers (DiT)** replace standard U-Net backbones with Transformer blocks to leverage scaling laws in generative vision pipelines.

bar_chart Throughput: DiT layouts achieve higher FID (Fréchet Inception Distance) quality scores than U-Net baselines at equivalent compute scales.
sections.codeBlock:
# Simple DDPM forward step (adding noise)
def forward_noising_process(x_0, alpha_bar, t):
    noise = np.random.normal(size=x_0.shape)
    # Compute noisy x_t = sqrt(alpha_bar_t) * x_0 + sqrt(1 - alpha_bar_t) * noise
    x_t = np.sqrt(alpha_bar[t]) * x_0 + np.sqrt(1.0 - alpha_bar[t]) * noise
    return x_t, noise

sections.title: Test-Time Compute Scaling: Search & Verifiers
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Rather than returning the first token-decoding output, reasoning models (e.g. o1 style) scale compute at inference time. The architecture routes queries through a **Chain of Thought**, generates multiple candidate reasoning branches, and utilizes **Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS)** or verifiers (PRMs) to prune incorrect pathways.

bar_chart Performance scaling: Scoring accuracy scales logarithmically with the amount of search compute spent at test time, offering a new scaling vector independent of training size.
sections.codeBlock:
# Best-of-N candidate selection using a verifier score
def best_of_n_inference(generator_model, verifier_model, prompt, n=8):
    candidates = [generator_model.generate(prompt) for _ in range(n)]
    # Score candidates using Process Reward Model
    scores = [verifier_model.score(cand) for cand in candidates]
    best_idx = np.argmax(scores)
    return candidates[best_idx], scores[best_idx]

sections.title: FAANG Staff+ Q&A: Emerging Architectures
sections.description:
Q: When should you use a Mamba/SSM model over a Transformer in production?
A: Choose Mamba/SSMs when: 1) Processing extremely long sequences (e.g. 100K+ context lengths of telemetry, codebases, or genomic sequences) where Transformer KV cache memory limits are exceeded. 2) Deploying on edge devices where memory bandwidth is restricted. Choose Transformers when: 1) The task demands multi-hop semantic cross-attention. 2) You need to leverage highly optimized hardware libraries (like FlashAttention) and mature compiler support.

Q: What is Flow Matching in generative modeling?
A: Flow Matching is a generative framework that generalizes diffusion models. Standard diffusion uses curved trajectories to map noise to data, which is slow to sample and requires many integration steps (e.g. 50-100). Flow Matching constructs straight vector fields (linear paths) between the noise and target, allowing models to generate high-quality outputs in fewer steps (e.g. 10-20), significantly improving serving speed.

details: Staff Engineer Summary: Evaluate models using a two-dimensional matrix: context length requirements and inference budget. For linear-scaling contexts, deploy SSMs (Mamba). For generative vision, utilize DiT. For complex logical queries, implement search-based test-time compute verifiers.
